seed_instruction: Write a story about a dropout who returns to school later in life to achieve their dreams.
org_constraint: Capitalize all instances of the word "DREAM" for emphasis.
new_constraint: The story must be written in lowercase, with no capitalized words.
instruction: Write a story about a dropout who returns to school later in life to achieve their dreams. Capitalize all instances of the word "DREAM" for emphasis. The story must be written in lowercase, with no capitalized words.
response:
<|channel>thought
*   Topic: A dropout returning to school later in life to achieve their dreams.
    *   Constraint 1: Capitalize all instances of the word "DREAM" (e.g., DREAM).
    *   Constraint 2: The entire story must be written in lowercase, *except* for the word "DREAM".
    *   Constraint 3: No other words should be capitalized.

    *   Character: Elias.
    *   Background: Dropped out of high school/college to work a manual labor job.
    *   Motivation: Always wanted to be an architect/engineer/artist.
    *   Action: Enrolls in a night school or community college in his 40s.
    *   Climax/Resolution: Studying hard, feeling the struggle, finally seeing the progress toward his DREAM.

    *   *Drafting sentence 1:* elias spent twenty years working in a warehouse.
    *   *Drafting sentence 2:* he had left school early because he needed to pay the bills.
    *   *Drafting sentence 3:* but every night, he would sit in his small apartment and sketch buildings in his head.
    *   *Drafting sentence 4:* it was his ultimate DREAM.
    *   *Drafting sentence 5:* at forty-five, he decided it was time to change.
    *   *Drafting sentence 6:* he signed up for a community college course in architecture.
    *   *Drafting sentence 7:* the other students were much younger, but he didn't care.
    *   *Drafting sentence 8:* he wanted to fulfill his DREAM.
    *   *Drafting sentence 9:* the math was hard, and his hands were stiff from years of labor.
    *   *Drafting sentence 10:* sometimes he felt like a fool, but then he would look at his sketches.
    *   *Drafting sentence 11:* they were the blueprints of his DREAM.
    *   *Drafting sentence 12:* he studied until the sun came up, fueled by coffee and determination.
    *   *Drafting sentence 13:* years passed, and slowly, the sketches became real designs.
    *   *Drafting sentence 14:* he realized that it is never too late to chase a DREAM.
    *   *Drafting sentence 15:* finally, he stood before a finished model, knowing he had achieved his DREAM.

    *   Check for any accidental capital letters (except for DREAM).
    *   Check if "DREAM" is capitalized correctly.
    *   Check the flow and tone.

    *Text:*
    elias spent twenty years working in a dusty warehouse, moving crates that felt heavier with every passing year. he had dropped out of high school to support his family, putting his education on a shelf that gathered dust. every night, however, he would sit at a rickety kitchen table and draw intricate blueprints of cities that did not yet exist. it was his secret DREAM.

    at forty-five, the weight of the crates began to ache in his bones more than his spirit. he decided that he would no longer let his life be defined by what he had left behind. he enrolled in a community college, sitting in a classroom filled with teenagers who looked at him with curiosity. he felt old, but his heart felt young. he was there to reclaim his DREAM.

    the textbooks were dense, and the equations were daunting. his hands, calloused from years of manual labor, struggled to hold the fine pens required for drafting. there were nights when he wanted to quit, when the exhaustion felt like a physical wall. but then he would look at his old sketches and remember why he started. he was chasing a DREAM that had lived in his mind for decades.

    he studied until the early hours of the morning, fueled by black coffee and a stubborn refusal to give up. he learned about structural integrity, aesthetics, and the way light dances across a facade. slowly, the sketches in his notebook began to transform into professional designs.

    one evening, as he stood before a finished model of a library, he felt a lump in his throat. he wasn't just a warehouse worker anymore. he was an architect. he had finally realized that it is never too late to pursue a DREAM. he had turned his long-held DREAM into a reality, proving that the best time to start is always now.
